summary: Recent updates to the graphics core of Firefox have focused on enhancing performance monitoring by removing outdated legacy mirrors for a large set of performance probes, which were no longer needed. In their place, the team has added telemetry alerting for several key high-level performance probes, enabling more efficient tracking and reporting of graphics performance metrics. This change is significant because it helps developers gain better insights into how the browser handles graphics rendering, ultimately leading to a smoother and more responsive user experience. **Improving the browser’s graphics performance matters for users who rely on high-quality visuals in applications and websites.**
